CheckTitle: AI Agent Identity Must Be Tagged and Inventoried
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Identity**. Verifies that every AI agent identity has the required governance tags (agent, owner, purpose) applied and is recorded in the organisation's identity inventory. Un-tagged identities cannot be attributed to a team, cannot be cost-allocated, and may survive after the owning team has disbanded.
Risk: Without required tags and owner attribution, orphaned agent identities accumulate over time, creating an unmanaged attack surface. Attackers can exploit forgotten identities that retain cloud permissions long after the agent is decommissioned.

Code.Other:
1. Add required tags (agent=true, owner=<team>, purpose=<use-case>) to the IAM role, managed identity, or service account used by the agent.
2. Register the identity in the organisation's CMDB or ASPM manifest.
3. Enforce tag policies (AWS Tag Policies, Azure Policy, GCP Labels) to prevent future untagged deployments.

Recommendation.Text: Apply mandatory governance tags to every agent identity and enforce tag compliance with cloud-native tag policies. Include agents in periodic identity review cycles.

Notes: ASPM-001 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
